item.title: Judge Calls US Government Ban on Anthropic AI Tools ‘Troubling’
item.description: A judge appeared skeptical of the Trump administration’s rationale for banning the federal government’s use of artificial intelligence technology from Anthropic PBC, a move that the Claude chatbot maker claims could cost it billions in lost revenue.

item.title: Oil Falls, US Stock Futures Climb on Iran Hopes: Markets Wrap
item.description: Oil fell and US equity futures climbed as optimism grew over Washington’s efforts to end the nearly month-long Middle East conflict.

item.title: Gold Holds Gain After Reports of US Negotiations to End Iran War
item.description: Gold steadied after snapping a nine-day decline, following reports that the US is pursuing talks with Iran to end the war in the Middle East even as it sends more troops to the region.

item.title: Fed’s Barr Says Rates May Need to Hold Steady for ‘Some Time’
item.description: Federal Reserve Governor Michael Barr said policymakers may need to keep interest rates steady for “some time” to address inflation that’s notably above the central bank’s 2% goal.

item.title: Chevron Warns California Risks Fuel Crisis Unless Iran War Eases
item.description: Chevron Corp. is warning that California is careening toward an energy crisis because of the Iran war and that the company may quit refining oil in the state unless officials roll back taxes and regulations.

item.title: Power Equipment Maker Forgent, Holders Offer 30 Million Shares
item.description: Forgent Power Solutions Inc. and some of its shareholders are offering to sell 30 million shares in aggregate, less than two months after the electrical equipment company’s initial public offering.

item.title: Oil Drops on Reports of US Diplomatic Push to End War With Iran
item.description: Oil fell on reports of a US diplomatic push to end the war with Iran, which eclipsed news that more troops would be sent to the region.

item.title: A Tiny Bank Runs Dry of Borrowers as Population Shrinks in Japan
item.description: Many Japanese banks in the countryside are struggling due to a shrinking population, but at a tiny credit union in the northernmost tip of the country, the situation is extreme.

item.title: Meta Deceived New Mexico Teens About Platform Safety, Jury Finds
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — A New Mexico jury ruled Tuesday that Meta knowingly harmed children’s mental health and concealed what it knew about child sexual exploitation on its social media platforms.

item.title: Bond Market Nears ‘Inflection Point’ After War-Related Selloff
item.description: A rush by bond traders to unwind US futures positions amid the selloff triggered by war in Iran is running its course, setting the stage for new wagers that will determine whether the rout reverses or deepens.

item.title: How Selling to a Repressive Regime Almost Broke a Tech Firm
item.description: Sandvine built a business offering network management tools that, in the wrong hands, could be used for censorship, until the US government intervened.

item.title: S. Korea Consumer Confidence Dips to 10-Month Low on Iran Risks
item.description: South Korea’s consumer confidence fell in March to the lowest in 10 months as the war in Iran darkened the outlook for growth and prices, tempering optimism from the elevated levels seen earlier this year.

item.title: Chile Holds Rates as Oil Surge Mars Inflation Outlook
item.description: Chile’s central bank held its key interest rate steady, warning that a coming jump in fuel prices will “significantly” boost inflation amid heightened uncertainty triggered by conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: BHP Sees Potash Heading Toward Deficit as Demand Outpaces Supply
item.description: BHP Group, the world’s largest mining company, expects the global potash market to tighten over the next decade as demand grows and geopolitical risks strain fertilizer supply chains.

item.title: Retail Traders Are Selling Stocks for First Time Since 2023
item.description: For retail investors, the US stock market’s most reliable dip buyers in recent years, risks are starting to outweigh the rewards.

item.title: OpenAI Plans to Discontinue Support for Sora AI Video Generator
item.description: OpenAI plans to discontinue its Sora video-generation service six months after debuting a standalone app, the company said on Tuesday.

item.title: AlphaSense Said to Seek Hundreds of Millions in Fresh Funding
item.description: Market-research startup AlphaSense is seeking hundreds of millions of dollars in fresh funding, according to people familiar with the matter, riding a wave of interest in artificial intelligence-powered data providers.

item.title: Iran Says Non-Hostile Ships Can Cross Hormuz on Its Terms
item.description: Iran said foreign ships are allowed to cross the Strait of Hormuz, as long as they aren’t supporting acts of aggression against the country and follow regulations put in place by Tehran.

item.title: Dollar Rally a ‘Bitter Triumph’ as White House Whims Overhang
item.description: During times of turmoil and spiking oil prices global investors often embrace the US currency as a steadying force, but the mercurial moods of the current administration and a shaky tech sector has weighed on the dollar’s advance, according to Barclays Plc.

item.title: Tether’s Landmark Fundraise on Hold as Crypto Giant Seeks Audit
item.description: Tether Holdings SA’s plans to raise as much as $20 billion are on pause as the stablecoin giant awaits the results of its first full financial audit, a step that may revive one of the most anticipated fundraisings in the digital-asset era.

item.title: Uber Bets on Three Competing Candidates for California Governor
item.description: Uber Technologies Inc. is backing three different candidates in the race for governor of California, hedging its political bets on a state that’s a bellwether for policy and technology shifts.

item.title: Amazon Acquires Fauna Robotics, Entering Consumer Humanoid Market
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. acquired New York-based startup Fauna Robotics, becoming the latest technology giant to step into the burgeoning consumer humanoid market.

item.title: Billionaire Qatari Sheikh, Amazon Back Robotics Startup Neura
item.description: Sheikh Hamad bin Jassim Al Thani, a billionaire member of the Qatari royal family, and Amazon.com Inc. have joined the latest funding round for German startup Neura Robotics.

item.title: Iran War Pulls Petrobras Into Brazil’s Inflation Battle
item.description: Latin America’s largest oil producer Petrobras is moving to contain war-fueled energy inflation in an election year, aligning with government efforts that could backfire and lead to diesel shortages in Brazil.

item.title: Apple Plans AI Reboot With Siri App, New Look and ‘Ask Siri’ Button in iOS 27
item.description: Apple Inc. is testing a standalone app for its Siri voice assistant alongside a new “Ask Siri” feature that will work across the company’s software, part of a broader artificial intelligence overhaul.

item.title: Circle Shares Fall Most Ever On Latest Stablecoin Rewards Move
item.description: Circle Internet Group Inc., the issuer of the USDC stablecoin, fell the most on record on Tuesday, with investors reacting to potential changes to stablecoin regulation that could make the firm’s cryptocurrency less attractive to large holders. Concerns that a competitor is readying a move into the US market also hurt Circle’s share price.

item.title: Palantir CTO Sees Iran War as First Major AI-Driven Conflict
item.description: Palantir Technologies Inc. Chief Technology Officer Shyam Sankar said that the war in Iran will likely be remembered as the first major conflict where artificial intelligence played a central role, representing a turning point in modern warfare.

item.title: Soaring Stocks Boosted Canada’s Crown-Jewel Pensions Amid PE Rut
item.description: Canada’s pensions have spent years betting that illiquid, private bets will yield superior returns. Lately, though, public markets have been doing the heavy lifting for them.

item.title: Another Hidden Inflation Driver
item.description: The inflation situation was already getting worse

item.title: Citadel Securities Nets Record $12 Billion Trading Haul in 2025
item.description: Citadel Securities posted a record $12.2 billion in trading revenue last year, as the market-making arm of billionaire Ken Griffin’s empire cements its status as a trading powerhouse.
